zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Ball於1545年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦時，她的父親Humfrey Ball22歲，母親Dorothy Beasley19歲。她於大約1594年與John Hussey-在英國英格蘭多瑟特艾德蒙善姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1665年8月18日在英國英格蘭多瑟特布利波特享年120歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特布利波特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Ball于1545年出生在英国英格兰倫敦时，她的父亲Humfrey Ball22岁，母亲Dorothy Beasley19岁。她于大约1594年与John Hussey-在英国英格兰多瑟特艾德蒙善姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1665年8月18日在英国英格兰多瑟特布利波特享年120岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特布利波特。

nameMeanings.name: Ball
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English bal, ball(e) ‘ball, sphere, globe, round body’ (Old French balle or Old English beall(a)), a nickname for a short, obese person.
English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a knoll or rounded hill, from the same Middle English word, bal(le) as in 1 above, but applied topographically.
English: from a Middle English adjective ball (weak form balle) in the sense ‘bald’, from ball ‘white streak, bald place’.

variants.name: Baller
meanings: English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English bal, ball(e) ‘ball, sphere’, perhaps denoting a ball maker.
English: variant of Ball 2 for someone who lived at a knoll, from a derivative of Middle English bal, ball(e) ‘knoll, rounded hill’, the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
German: from a variant of the ancient Germanic personal name Balther (from bald ‘bold, strong’).

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Corina, Elidoro, Fernando, Geraldo, Humberto, Joaquin, Ruben.

variants.name: Balls
meanings: English (Norfolk, Suffolk, and Essex): variant of Ball , with excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Ball was born in 1545, in London, England, United Kingdom, her father, Humfrey Ball, was 22 and her mother, Dorothy Beasley, was 19. She married John Hussey- about 1594, in Edmondsham, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 18 August 1665, in Bridport, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 120, and was buried in Bridport, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Ball est née en 1545, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Humfrey Ball, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Beasley, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé John Hussey- environ 1594, à Edmondsham, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 18 août 1665, à Bridport, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 120 ans, et a été enterrée à Bridport, Dorset, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Ball(이)가 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Humfrey Ball(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Beasley(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1594년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 에드몽샤에서 John Hussey-(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1665년 8월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 브리드포트에서 120세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 브리드포트에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John Hussey- aproximadamente 1594, em Edmondsham, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 18 de agosto de 1665, em Bridport, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 120 anos, e foi sepultada em Bridport, Dorset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Ball родилась в 1545 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Humfrey Ball было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Beasley, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hussey- около 1594 г. в местности эдмондшам, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 18 августа 1665 г. в местности бридпорт, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 120 лет и была похоронена в местности бридпорт, дорсет, Англия.
